title: atriplex hortensis subsp. microtheca
intro: Atriplex hortensis subsp. microtheca, also known as inland saltbush, is a subspecies of the ornamental plant Atriplex hortensis. This shrub-like plant is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Atriplex hortensis subsp. microtheca include its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, burns, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and its leaves and stems can be dried and used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
